input.background: The time to failure of a component has a gamma distribution with $\alpha =3$ and $\beta=5$.
input.question: Explain the other form of the gamma probability density function and its characteristics.

output.answer: The other form of the gamma probability density function can be written as follows: $$ f(t)=\frac{\beta^\alpha t^{\alpha-1}}{\Gamma(\alpha)} e^{-t \beta} \quad t>0 $$ This pdf is characterized by two parameters: shape parameter $\alpha$ and scale parameter $\beta$. When $0<\alpha<1$, the failure rate monotonically decreases; when $\alpha>1$, the failure rate monotonically increases; when $\alpha=1$, the failure rate is constant.

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
